name: Traditional Southern Buttermilk Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F. In a large bowl, sift together flour, salt, baking soda, and baking powder.
Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut the shortening into the flour mixture until coarse crumbs form.
2. Gradually add buttermilk, tossing with a fork, until a dough forms.
3. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface. Gather it into a disk.
Knead lightly a few times until smooth.
(The dough may be made up to two hours ahead, wrapped in plastic wrap,
and refrigerated until ready to use.)
4. Pat the dough to 3/4 inch thick. Using a biscuit cutter or a glass dipped in flour, cut out biscuits.
Place them, about two inches apart, onto an ungreased baking sheet.
Gather any remaining dough, pat it to 3/4 inch thick, and cut out more biscuits.
5. Bake until golden brown, about 12-15 minutes. Serve hot.
